what_it_does: A stdlib-only shared HTTP server shell for local speech-to-text ear wrappers. It exposes POST /inference (multipart file= upload, with a raw-bytes fallback), GET / and /health liveness, delegates audio transcription to a wrapper-supplied callable, optionally runs a warm() hook before serving, and provides a WAV-to-mono-16kHz-float32 helper.
observations: Top-level imports are stdlib-only; heavy libraries are loaded lazily inside wrapper functions or wav_bytes_to_float32.
The multipart parser is hand-written because cgi was removed in Python 3.13.
If the Content-Type is not multipart, the entire POST body is treated as raw audio bytes.
Liveness endpoints return 200 JSON and never raise.
Errors are surfaced as 500 JSON with the exception type and message.
warm() is invoked before serve_forever() so model-loading/CUDA failures happen at startup.
Request logging is silenced by overriding log_message.
wav_bytes_to_float32 imports numpy and soundfile inside the function and performs simple linear resampling to 16 kHz.
standouts: Hand-rolled, dependency-free multipart boundary parser to stay stdlib-only across Python versions.
Fail-loud 500 error responses that mirror the engine service contract.
Optional warm() hook to force eager model loading and early failure.
Uniform /inference route shape intended to match whisper.cpp so one client path serves all ears.
conventions: Fail-loud error handling with type: message payloads.
Lazy import of heavy scientific/audio libraries.
Mirrors an external service contract rather than inventing a new one.
concerns: The ad-hoc multipart parser may mishandle quoted boundaries, CRLF edge cases, content-transfer-encoding, or malformed parts.
Treating any non-multipart body as raw audio could mask client mistakes.
Linear interpolation resampling is simple but not high-fidelity.
There is no request size limit, authentication, or graceful shutdown mechanism.
Silencing log_message removes request logs that could aid debugging.
notes: Stdlib HTTP shell that lets every STT ear wrapper expose the same /inference contract as whisper.cpp while keeping heavy dependencies out of the shared file.
questions: What are the exact per-ear port assignments?
Is there a maximum allowed request or audio size?
Does the wrapper-supplied transcribe callable need to be thread-safe?
What happens if warm() raises before serve_forever()?
Are there tests covering the multipart parser's edge cases?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a shared, stdlib-only HTTP shell with a uniform /inference contract and fail-loud behavior; the code implements exactly that, including the multipart parser and liveness endpoints.
intention_signals: Decouple the HTTP transport for local STT services from each ear's ASR implementation so a single client path can reach custom wrappers and whisper.cpp alike, while keeping the shared module cheap to import.
novelty: A hand-written stdlib multipart/form-data boundary parser designed to avoid the removed cgi module and preserve Python 3.13+ compatibility without adding dependencies.
contribution: Provides a reusable, dependency-free HTTP service template and multipart parser that any STT wrapper can mount, plus a WAV-to-float32 conversion helper, enabling one client code path for multiple local STT backends.
summary_for_embedding: A stdlib-only HTTP server shell for local speech-to-text ear wrappers that exposes /inference via multipart file upload and /health liveness, delegating transcription to a supplied callable and keeping heavy ASR dependencies out of the shared module.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to let voice/stt.py reach every local STT ear, including custom wrappers and whisper.cpp, through one uniform HTTP contract without editing transport code per provider.

symbols.does: Extracts uploaded file bytes from a multipart/form-data POST without external dependencies.
symbols.description: Parses the Content-Type boundary and iterates parts to find a file upload part, falling back to recognized field names; if the request is not multipart it returns the raw body as audio bytes. Raises if no usable file field is found.

symbols.does: Runs the shared STT HTTP service on a wrapper-supplied port.
symbols.description: Builds a ThreadingHTTPServer-backed handler class, optionally calls warm() to load the model before listening, prints a readiness line to stderr, and serves forever on 127.0.0.1.

symbols.does: Inner BaseHTTPRequestHandler subclass implementing the STT HTTP contract.
symbols.description: Defines the request handler with liveness GET routes, a POST /inference route that parses audio and calls the wrapper's transcribe callable, and fail-loud 500 error responses.

symbols.does: Helper that writes an HTTP response with a given status, body, and content type.
symbols.description: Encodes string bodies if needed, sets Content-Type and Content-Length headers, and writes the response to the client.

symbols.does: Handles GET / and /health as liveness checks.
symbols.description: Returns a 200 JSON payload with ok:true and the ear name for the root and health paths; returns 404 with empty JSON for unknown paths.

symbols.does: Handles POST /inference by parsing audio and returning transcription text.
symbols.description: Validates the path, extracts audio via _parse_multipart_file, rejects empty audio, invokes transcribe(audio), and returns JSON {text: ...}; any exception becomes a 500 JSON error.
Overrides BaseHTTPRequestHandler.log_message with a no-op so the service does not emit access logs to stderr.

symbols.does: Converts WAV bytes to mono float32 numpy audio at 16 kHz.
symbols.description: Uses soundfile to read audio from a bytes buffer, mixes multi-channel to mono, and linearly resamples to 16000 Hz if needed; heavy imports happen inside the function.
